name: GoTo Group
sector: Indonesia tech — Gojek (on-demand), Tokopedia/TikTok Shop JV, GoTo Financial

evidence.title: GoTo group adjusted EBITDA positive; cost cuts ahead of plan
TikTok-Tokopedia e-commerce JV scaling GMV post-merger
GoPay standalone app gains traction; lending yields strong
On-demand competition with Grab pressures mobility take-rate
FY25 gross revenue Rp16.5T; share buyback extended

earnings_video.title: GoTo Group FY25 Results Briefing
earnings_video.quote: We achieved group adjusted EBITDA breakeven a quarter ahead of guidance, and GoPay is now our fastest-growing engagement driver.

analyst_steps.query: GoTo FY25 adjusted EBITDA cost rationalisation
analyst_steps.observation: Group adj EBITDA positive; fixed-cost base cut ~30% over 2 years

analyst_steps.query: TikTok Tokopedia JV GMV contribution GoTo
analyst_steps.observation: JV monetisation improving; GoTo books e-commerce service fee, not losses
GoPay MTU growth strong; consumer lending yields attractive, NPLs contained

analyst_steps.query: GoTo Gojek mobility competition Grab take rate
analyst_steps.observation: Mobility take-rate under pressure; offset by fintech mix shift

thesis.summary: GoTo's cost discipline got it to EBITDA breakeven early, and offloading e-commerce losses into the TikTok JV de-risks the model. GoPay + lending is the real growth engine. But mobility competition with Grab caps near-term upside, so the risk/reward is balanced rather than compelling at current levels.
risks: Mobility take-rate erosion from sustained Grab competition
Reliance on the TikTok-Tokopedia JV outside GoTo's full control
Rupiah volatility and Indonesian consumer-credit cycle risk
